shape_decode: cmp=W, vp=W, vpc=_, naive=L — cmplog AND value_profile each independently resolve the gate; naive blocks. A parallel-solvable gate where I2S substitution and VP Hamming/structural-gradient are redundant routes to the SAME target the branch needs.
label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern (cmp W, vp W, naive L). The evidence test is the INDEPENDENT corpus-content verification (G2): confirm BOTH resolving fuzzers' corpora carry the target the gate needs (a fixed-offset literal, or accumulated structure) relative to naive, WITHOUT consulting the resolve pattern itself.
round0_postmortem: Round-0 validated 6 branches (3818,3864,3951,4085 cleanly; the FOURCC pair 3811/3890 and the structural 14563 fell to inconclusive=rule_not_met). Re-inspecting the ACTUAL cached metrics shows the round-0 rules were MIS-CALIBRATED, not the mechanism refuted: (i) libpng_3811/3890 (sPLT FOURCC) — VP signed_target_enrich=0.355 (<0.5) only because naive's baseline target_frac is already high (~0.695) at the chunk-type window, compressing the log2 ratio; VP's decoy_enrich=-17.582 is IDENTICAL to the strongly-validated 3864 branch (VP crushed the decoy byte), and VP target_frac 0.825 > naive 0.695. The parallel-literal mechanism is POSITIVELY supported; the signed_target_enrich>0.5 threshold was the defect. (ii) sqlite3_14563 (structural) — round-0 required tag_lift>=1.3 AND size_lift>=1.3; the VP arm returned size_lift=2.12 (decisive) but tag_lift=1.27 (right at the n_W=3 noise floor), so the AND narrowly failed despite a 2x size accumulation; cmplog arm is overwhelming (size_lift 4.41, tag_lift 3.4). Again a threshold defect, not a refutation. (iii) bloaty_57 — cmplog enrich 10.528 confirmed, but the VP-vs-naive OE study was never run for bloaty (no row in arb_operand_enrich_vp), so the VP arm is MEASUREMENT-UNAVAILABLE, not refuted. (iv) libxml2_6785 — no_gate_signature both arms: the gate is CUR==0 at a MOVING parse cursor (input-dependent offset), which no built fixed-offset tool can place — genuinely undecidable.

hypotheses.label: A fixed-offset 1-4 byte VALUE literal/enum gates the branch; both I2S (direct substitution) and VP (Hamming/prefix gradient) drive the gate window toward the target value AND away from the decoy, so BOTH resolving corpora are enriched in the target and depleted in the decoy at the gate offset relative to naive.

params.note: Score the cmplog arm (signed_target_enrich/decoy_enrich, cmplog-vs-naive OE_CACHE) AND the value_profile arm (vp_signed_target_enrich/vp_decoy_enrich, vp-vs-naive OE_VP_CACHE) independently against naive. Per-branch gate offset+target/decoy bytes come from the signature: bloaty_57 e_machine offset 0x12-0x13 target 0x3e; libpng_3864/3951/4085 IHDR color_type offset 0x19 target 0x03 (decoy 0x00/0x04/0x06); libpng_3811/3890 chunk-type offset target 'sPLT' 73 50 4c 54 (decoy other FOURCC); libpng_3818 chunk-validation window.

hypotheses.prediction: Each resolving fuzzer's corpus drives the gate window toward the target literal: EITHER it over-represents the target byte vs naive (signed_target_enrich > 0.5) OR it depletes the decoy byte vs naive (decoy_enrich < -1.0) — the two are complementary corpus-content footprints of the same drive (target enrichment saturates low when naive's baseline target fraction is already high, as at the libpng chunk-type window; decoy depletion is then the cleaner signal). DISCRIMINATING: a structural/length gate returns no_gate_signature (caught instead by parallel_structural_accumulation_gate); a single-technique-only gate drives the window for ONLY ONE of {cmplog, value_profile}, leaving the other arm at enrich~0 / decoy~0 (the sqlite3-structural and bloaty-VP-missing cases fail HERE precisely because only one arm clears); a non-causal coincidence drives neither. Only the parallel-literal mechanism makes BOTH arms drive the SAME gate window.
hypotheses.g2_independence: operand_enrichment is computed over saved-corpus byte frequencies at the gate offset; it never reads the per-trial resolve/block label that defined the shape. Confirming both fuzzers' corpora drive the same gate window (toward target / away from decoy) is an independent corpus-content signal. The refined rule changed only the per-arm THRESHOLD (added the complementary decoy-depletion clause); it did not relax the requirement that BOTH arms drive the gate.

hypotheses.label: The gate is NOT a single fixed-offset literal but a structural/length/accumulation condition (repeated integer-token body, or a multi-comparison validation cascade). Both I2S and VP help by accumulating target tokens / growing the seed, so the resolving seeds are LARGER and carry MORE target tokens than the blocking seeds — there is no single alignable gate byte.

params.note: Aim joint_necessity at the CONSERVATIVE arm pair value_profile-resolving vs naive-blocking (NOT cmplog, which is overwhelming: size_lift 4.41 / tag_lift 3.4 / n_W=10). If the harder-to-credit VP gradient-feedback arm STILL shows the seeds growing and accumulating SQL integer-literal tokens (observed size_lift=2.12, token_lift=1.27, n_W=3), the parallel-structural-accumulation claim is well supported — cmplog trivially accumulates too. Tokens are sqlite-specific ASCII digit '5'(0x35), operators '+'/'-'/'/' and the SELECT keyword.

hypotheses.prediction: size_lift >= 1.5 (the value_profile-resolving seeds are materially larger than naive-blocking — structural accumulation, not a single-byte flip) AND token_lift >= 1.2 (they carry more integer-literal/operator tokens). DISCRIMINATING: a fixed-offset literal gate would show size_lift ~ 1.0 (same-sized seeds differing at one byte) and be caught by parallel_fixed_offset_literal_gate's enrichment instead; absence of size/token lift means neither technique is accumulating structure and the branch falls to fallback. The rule LEADS on size_lift (the robust signal at small n_W) with a lenient token_lift floor (round-0's tag_lift>=1.3 narrowly clipped the real 1.27 at n_W=3).
hypotheses.g2_independence: Seed size + token counts are intrinsic corpus-content properties of the resolving vs blocking seed sets; they do not consult the resolve label. byte_freq_ratio is forbidden here by data_realities (different-sized seeds defeat byte-alignment), which is why this structural subtype is kept separate from hypothesis 1 and verified with struct_size_and_token_count.

decidable_false.reason: The gate is `CUR == 0` at libxml2's MOVING parse cursor (the NUL byte must land at a position determined by parse state after optional 4-byte encoding detection), so there is NO FIXED input offset for any built tool to place. operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature for both arms (confirmed in cache); value_distance_reached needs a fixed operand window AND its scope_limits flag width<=2 operands (here width=1, a single NUL) as chance-alignment-prone. No built measurement can discriminate the parallel-NUL mechanism from coincidence for a moving 1-byte gate. This is a gate-SHAPE limitation (true even if libxml2 corpus were local to this server), not a corpus-availability gap. Honest decidable:false per G3 — do not invent a measurement the data cannot support.

expected_outcomes_on_current_cache._doc: Replayed against the actual arbiter caches (arb_operand_enrich_{s4,sB}.csv + _vp_ + a fresh joint_necessity run) so the orchestrator can sanity-check the re-arbitration. NOT authoritative — the arbiter recomputes.

data_realities_respected.corpus_availability: operand_enrichment (corpus_queue) + joint_necessity (seed_set) need on-disk corpus / resolving+blocking seeds. On THIS shape the OE caches already hold libpng + bloaty(cmplog) + libxml2(skip) rows from sB and sqlite3 from s4, so the rules ARE scorable for those targets. bloaty_57's VP arm is the only genuine measurement gap (the vp-vs-naive OE study was not run for bloaty) — left at inconclusive/fallback, NOT decidable:false, since it is recoverable by running the VP-arm OE study on sB.
data_realities_respected.i2s_untagged: No descriptor relies on a tagged 'I2S event' in seed_lineage — both descriptors are corpus-content measurements (byte enrichment / size+token), the only feasible way to observe I2S and VP per data_realities.
data_realities_respected.byte_alignment_only_for_fixed_gates: byte_freq_ratio (H1) applies ONLY to fixed-offset value gates (enum/FOURCC). The structural sqlite3 branch is routed to struct_size_and_token_count; the moving-cursor libxml2 branch is decidable:false — neither is byte-aligned, per the different-sized-seed / moving-offset realities.

notes.what_changed_from_round0: Two THRESHOLD recalibrations on the SAME tools (path (a)), each justified by the actual returned metrics, plus one honest decidable:false. (1) H1 rule: round-0 required `cmplog.signed_target_enrich > 0.5 AND value_profile.signed_target_enrich > 0.5`. Added the complementary per-arm clause `OR decoy_enrich < -1.0`, because the FOURCC branches (3811/3890) have VP decoy depletion -17.582 (identical to validated 3864) but a log2 target-enrich compressed to 0.355 by naive's high baseline target fraction. Decoy depletion is a positive, baseline-insensitive footprint of the same drive. This recovers 3811/3890 WITHOUT flipping bloaty (VP arm missing -> still fails) or sqlite3 (VP enrich 0.089 / decoy -0.22 -> still fails H1, correctly routes to H2). (2) H2 rule: round-0 `size_lift>=1.3 AND tag_lift>=1.3` narrowly clipped sqlite3_14563's real VP-arm tag_lift 1.27 at n_W=3 despite a 2.12x size accumulation; lead on size_lift>=1.5 with a lenient token_lift>=1.2 floor, aimed at the conservative VP-vs-naive arm.
notes.merges: Same 2 discriminable hypotheses as round-0. H1 (fixed-offset literal) merges 1-2B enum + 4B FOURCC: operand_enrichment measures target-window over-representation / decoy depletion identically regardless of literal width — no real-data measurement separates a 1B enum from a 4B FOURCC, so keeping them apart would be an undiscriminable width distinction. H2 (structural accumulation) merges validation-cascade + repeated-token: both are variable-sized accumulation gates that operand_enrichment skips and struct_size_and_token_count measures the same way (resolving seeds bigger / more tokens); a cascade vs a token count are mechanistically distinct but the size+token measurement cannot tell them apart, so they collapse.
notes.discrimination_between_hypotheses: H1 and H2 are mutually discriminable: a fixed-offset literal yields per-gate-offset enrichment/depletion with size_lift~1.0; a structural gate returns no_gate_signature for OE but size_lift/token_lift>1. decision_order tries the fixed-offset enrichment first; on no_gate_signature it falls to the structural test. (Note: round-0's `members` routing was advisory — the arbiter applies BOTH rules per branch in decision_order and takes the first that holds; e.g. it validated libpng_3818 under H1 even though round-0 listed it under H2. Membership here is documentation, not a hard route.)
notes.g3_honesty: libxml2_6785 is decidable:false on a gate-SHAPE ground (moving parse cursor -> no fixed offset any built tool can place), not on off-server corpus. bloaty_57 is left INCONCLUSIVE (fallback), not decidable:false, because its VP arm is merely un-run (recoverable) while its cmplog arm is a confirmed 10.528 enrichment. No mechanism was invented to force a label on any branch.
notes.targets: bloaty (1: 57 — fixed-offset, cmplog-confirmed, VP arm pending), libpng (6: 3811,3818,3864,3890,3951,4085 — all fixed-offset H1), libxml2 (1: 6785 — decidable:false, moving cursor), sqlite3 (1: 14563 — structural H2).
